seo.title: Keeping Alternatives: Find the Best E-commerce Customer Support Solution
seo.description: Looking for a Keeping alternative? Compare top e-commerce help desks like Replyma, Gorgias, Zendesk, and Tidio to find the perfect fit for your store's...

content.intro: Keeping is a solid customer support solution, especially known for its e-commerce focus and returns management features. However, growing e-commerce businesses often find they need more advanced AI capabilities and a broader ecosystem to handle increasing customer inquiries and complex support scenarios efficiently. This page explores the best Keeping alternatives to help you find the perfect fit for your online store.
why_look_for_alternatives: **Limited AI Resolution:** Keeping's AI might not resolve a high percentage of tickets automatically, forcing your team to handle more manual work, especially as order volumes increase during peak seasons.
**Scalability Concerns:** While suitable for smaller stores, Keeping might not scale as effectively for larger e-commerce operations with complex needs and high ticket volumes.

**Integration Limitations:** Compared to larger players like Gorgias and Zendesk, Keeping offers fewer integrations with essential e-commerce tools and platforms. This can create data silos and hinder workflow automation.
**Brand Recognition:** Being a smaller brand, Keeping's ecosystem and community support might not be as robust as alternatives like Replyma or Zendesk.

alternatives.name: Replyma
alternatives.tagline: AI-powered customer support that resolves tickets automatically.
alternatives.description: Replyma is an AI-first customer support platform built for e-commerce. It leverages advanced AI to automatically resolve a high percentage of customer inquiries, freeing up your team to focus on complex issues. With built-in RAG and knowledge base, Replyma offers a comprehensive solution for e-commerce businesses looking to scale their support operations. Self-hosted real-time options are available.
alternatives.best_for: E-commerce businesses seeking to automate customer support and reduce ticket volume.
alternatives.pricing: Custom pricing based on ticket volume and features.
pros: High AI ticket resolution rate, reducing manual workload.
Built-in RAG and knowledge base for efficient knowledge management.
Self-hosted real-time option for enhanced security and control.
cons: May require some initial setup and training to fully leverage AI capabilities.
Custom pricing can be less transparent than fixed-price options.

alternatives.name: Gorgias
alternatives.tagline: The leading customer service platform for e-commerce.
alternatives.description: Gorgias is a popular customer service platform designed specifically for e-commerce businesses. It integrates seamlessly with Shopify, Magento, and other platforms, offering a centralized view of customer data and enabling personalized support experiences. Ideal for brands that want to manage all their customer interactions in one place.
alternatives.best_for: Shopify and Magento stores looking for a comprehensive help desk solution.
pros: Deep integration with Shopify and other e-commerce platforms.
Automation rules and macros to streamline workflows.
Multi-channel support (email, live chat, social media).
cons: Can be expensive for smaller businesses with limited support volume.
AI capabilities are less advanced than AI-first solutions like Replyma.

alternatives.name: Zendesk
alternatives.tagline: Powerful customer service software for businesses of all sizes.
alternatives.description: Zendesk is a versatile customer service platform that can be adapted for e-commerce. It offers a wide range of features, including ticketing, knowledge base, and live chat. While not exclusively focused on e-commerce, Zendesk's flexibility makes it a suitable option for businesses with diverse support needs.
alternatives.best_for: Businesses that need a scalable and customizable customer service solution.
pros: Highly customizable and scalable to meet evolving needs.
Extensive integration options with various business tools.
Robust reporting and analytics capabilities.
cons: Can be complex to set up and manage, requiring dedicated resources.
Not specifically tailored for e-commerce workflows, requiring custom configurations.

alternatives.name: Tidio
alternatives.tagline: All-in-one customer service platform with live chat and chatbots.
alternatives.description: Tidio is a customer service platform that combines live chat, email, and chatbots to provide instant support to website visitors. It is easy to set up and use, making it a good option for small e-commerce businesses looking to improve customer engagement and generate leads.
alternatives.best_for: Small e-commerce businesses looking for an affordable and easy-to-use live chat solution.
alternatives.pricing: Free plan available; paid plans start at $29/month.
pros: Easy to set up and use, even for non-technical users.
Affordable pricing, including a free plan.
Combines live chat, email, and chatbots in one platform.
cons: Limited advanced features compared to more comprehensive platforms.
AI chatbot capabilities are not as sophisticated as dedicated AI solutions like Replyma.

alternatives.name: Help Scout
alternatives.tagline: Customer support software for growing businesses.
alternatives.description: Help Scout is a customer support platform focused on providing a simple and collaborative support experience. It features shared inboxes, knowledge base, and reporting tools to help teams manage customer inquiries efficiently. A good option for e-commerce businesses that value simplicity and ease of use.
alternatives.best_for: E-commerce businesses that value simplicity and collaboration in their support workflows.
pros: Simple and intuitive interface.
Collaborative features for team-based support.
Knowledge base functionality to empower self-service.
cons: Limited automation capabilities compared to other platforms.
Fewer integrations with e-commerce platforms than specialized solutions like Gorgias.

alternatives.name: Re:amaze
alternatives.tagline: Customer service and engagement platform for e-commerce.
alternatives.description: Re:amaze is a customer service platform specifically designed for e-commerce businesses. It offers a range of features, including live chat, email, social media integration, and chatbots. It is a solid all-in-one solution for brands that want to manage all their customer interactions in one place.
alternatives.best_for: E-commerce businesses that want a comprehensive customer service solution with a focus on engagement.
pros: Designed specifically for e-commerce workflows.
Multi-channel support with live chat, email, and social media integration.
Chatbot capabilities for automated customer interactions.
cons: Can be more expensive than simpler solutions.
The chatbot builder might not be as intuitive as dedicated AI chatbot platforms.

alternatives.name: Freshdesk
alternatives.tagline: Customer support software for businesses of all sizes.
alternatives.description: Freshdesk is a popular customer support platform that offers a wide range of features, including ticketing, knowledge base, and automation. While not exclusively focused on e-commerce, Freshdesk's versatility makes it a suitable option for businesses with diverse support needs.
alternatives.best_for: Businesses that need a feature-rich and affordable customer support solution.
alternatives.pricing: Free plan available; paid plans start at $15/agent/month.
pros: Affordable pricing, including a free plan.
Wide range of features, including ticketing, knowledge base, and automation.
Integration with various business tools.
cons: Can be complex to set up and manage, especially for smaller businesses.
Not specifically tailored for e-commerce workflows.

alternatives.name: Zoho Desk
alternatives.tagline: Customer service software for businesses of all sizes.
alternatives.description: Zoho Desk is a customer service platform that offers a range of features, including ticketing, knowledge base, and automation. It integrates seamlessly with other Zoho products, making it a good option for businesses that already use the Zoho ecosystem. Works well with e-commerce.
alternatives.best_for: Businesses that already use the Zoho ecosystem and need a customer service solution.
alternatives.pricing: Free plan available; paid plans start at $14/agent/month.
pros: Integration with other Zoho products.
Affordable pricing, including a free plan.
Automation capabilities for streamlined workflows.
cons: Can be overwhelming for businesses that don't already use Zoho products.
Not specifically tailored for e-commerce workflows.

alternatives.name: Kustomer
alternatives.description: Kustomer (now owned by Meta) is a CRM platform built for customer service. It offers a unified view of customer data and enables personalized support experiences. It is well suited for e-commerce brands that want to build long-term relationships with their customers.
alternatives.best_for: E-commerce businesses that want to build long-term customer relationships.
pros: Unified view of customer data.
Personalized support experiences.
CRM capabilities for building long-term relationships.
cons: Can be expensive, especially for smaller businesses.
May require dedicated resources to manage and optimize.

alternatives.name: Intercom
alternatives.tagline: Customer messaging platform for sales, marketing, and support.
alternatives.description: Intercom is a customer messaging platform that offers a range of features, including live chat, email, and chatbots. It is a good option for e-commerce businesses that want to engage with customers throughout the entire customer journey, from initial website visit to post-purchase support.
alternatives.best_for: E-commerce businesses that want to engage with customers throughout the entire customer journey.
pros: Engages customers throughout the entire customer journey.
Live chat, email, and chatbot capabilities.
Marketing and sales automation features.
cons: Can be expensive, especially for businesses with a large customer base.
Can be complex to set up and manage, requiring dedicated resources.

faq.question: What are the key differences between Replyma and Keeping?
faq.answer: Replyma is an AI-first platform designed to automate a large portion of customer support tickets. Replyma offers built-in RAG and knowledge base features. Keeping, while focused on e-commerce, might require more manual intervention from support agents and has fewer advanced AI capabilities.

faq.question: Is Gorgias a better option than Keeping for Shopify stores?
faq.answer: Gorgias offers deeper integration with Shopify, making it a strong contender for stores heavily invested in the Shopify ecosystem. However, consider Replyma if you want to prioritize AI-driven automation and ticket resolution.

faq.question: Which alternative is best for small e-commerce businesses with limited budgets?
faq.answer: Tidio offers a free plan and affordable paid plans, making it a good option for small businesses. However, its AI capabilities are less advanced. Freshdesk also offers a free plan, but its setup can be more complex.

faq.question: How does Replyma's AI compare to the AI offered by other customer support platforms?
faq.answer: Replyma is designed with a focus on AI-driven automation. It aims to resolve a higher share of tickets automatically compared to platforms like Keeping, Gorgias, or Zendesk, which typically use AI to augment human agents.

faq.question: What factors should I consider when choosing a Keeping alternative?
faq.answer: Consider your budget, the size and complexity of your e-commerce operation, your need for AI-driven automation, and the importance of integrations with other e-commerce tools and platforms. If you're dealing with a high volume of repetitive inquiries, an AI-first solution like Replyma can significantly reduce your workload.

content.cta_text: Ready to automate your e-commerce customer support? See how Replyma can resolve more tickets automatically.
